Curl request analysis and Wikipedia MD5 path calculation logic show the URL is fabricated.

privileged_info_summary: The expected output paths for the PsychoPy experiment (/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/ttc_task.psyexp), conditions CSV (/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/conditions.csv), and background image (/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/road_bg.jpg) exactly match the task description and verifier logic. However, the provided image_url (https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/P1010368.JPG/1280px-P1010368.JPG) is fabricated and perpetually returns a 404 Not Found error because the path '1/18' mathematically contradicts the MD5 hash of 'P1010368.JPG'. Agents will have to save the 404 HTML response as a .jpg to satisfy the lenient file existence check in the verifier.
